message:
chore(beads): close ft-ixgqo — writer_loop block_on bridge removed

Production block_on at storage.rs:11574 replaced with poll-and-park
try_recv + sleep(1ms). 47 storage tests pass post-fix. Implementation
landed at 3f0139bde.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -345,7 +345,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-iqwt5","title":"perf(storage): avoid per-pane no-op FTS catchup on healthy startup","description":"Hot path: crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:16959-16983 lists every distinct pane_id and calls sync_fts_for_pane for each pane on every healthy startup. sync_fts_for_pane then does progress lookup plus an unindexed-segment query even when the pane has no new output, making startup O(number_of_panes) SQL round trips for a fully caught-up FTS index. Full rebuild has the same pane list pattern at crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:16813-16837, where it is expected, but incremental healthy startup can be narrowed.

Concrete optimization: replace the incremental pane list with a single query that joins output_segments against fts_pane_progress and returns only panes where MAX(seq) is greater than last_indexed_seq or progress is absent. Keep the seq=0 first-pane sentinel behavior by including panes with no progress. Add a regression test with many caught-up panes and one dirty pane asserting sync_fts_for_pane is invoked only for the dirty/missing-progress set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"greyfalcon","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:33:17.375376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:09:34.076966Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:09:34.076705Z","close_reason":"Selective FTS startup sync landed in 6a0590d2 and targeted regression passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ismux","title":"[mission][dispatch] MissionDispatcher reports accepted dispatch without calling WorkflowRunner::handle_detection","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:200-226`, `dispatch_single()` builds a synthetic detection, then only calls `runner.find_matching_workflow(&detection)` and fabricates `accepted: true` plus a synthetic `execution_id` when a workflow exists. It never invokes `WorkflowRunner::handle_detection()`, so no lock acquisition, no concurrency-limit enforcement, no storage row, and no real workflow start happen.

Impact: callers receive a success-shaped `DispatchResult` that claims work was accepted even though the runner was never asked to start anything. This also makes the existing close reason on `ft-y9lnb.3` overstated relative to the current code.

Fix direction: bridge the sync mission loop to the async runner start path with a bounded runtime, map the actual `WorkflowStartResult` into `DispatchResult`, and only emit started metadata from a real start result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:05:13.673841Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.575203Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.574685Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-4r2ib. crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs is already dirty in this checkout with the exact handle_detection/WorkflowStartResult fix and regression coverage, so I did not collide with pane-4-owned WIP.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-iwlsh","title":"[perf][scan-pipeline] scan_locate allocates a per-call Vec on the chunked hot path; the caller never needs it","description":"Audit-pass profiling finding (AmberFox 2026-04-26). scan_pipeline.rs:394 is the ONLY production caller of TriggerScanner::scan_locate (the other 4 callers are inside #[cfg(test)] blocks). The call shape is:

  for matched in self.trigger_scanner.scan_locate(scratch) {
      if matched.offset.saturating_add(matched.length) <= overlap_len {
          continue;
      }
      ...
  }

scan_locate (pattern_trigger.rs:589-595) is a thin wrapper that allocates a fresh Vec::new() and then calls for_each_leftmost_match to push matches into it; the caller iterates and consumes the vec. The Vec is never retained. For high-match-rate inputs (compiler output with thousands of warnings, log streams full of ERROR markers) this is O(matches) allocation traffic on the same hot path ft-6db1t addressed for the trigger-counts HashMap.

Fix: rewrite scan_pipeline.rs:394 to use for_each_leftmost_match directly with a closure that performs the offset filter + array-indexed increment. Eliminates the per-call Vec allocation entirely. The wrapper scan_locate stays for the 4 test callers + external API.

Same proven pattern as ft-6db1t (TriggerCategoryCounts replacing HashMap<TriggerCategory, u64>): closure-driven hot path, allocation kept in the test-only convenience method.

Estimated impact: per-chunk allocation eliminated. On high-frequency capture paths (200+-pane fleets, log-heavy panes) this is amortized but non-trivial.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:44:01.861265Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:07:47.133725Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:07:47.133244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-ixgqo","title":"[ft-3tvvt] remove production storage writer_loop block_on bridge","description":"Follow-on from ft-3tvvt. storage.rs writer_loop creates a private current-thread runtime to block_on runtime_async::mpsc::Receiver::recv. Refactor to a small writer inbox bridge with a synchronous receive API, or move the writer loop to an async-owned confinement design, without direct tokio usage.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:00:14.615961Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T21:03:42.025633Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-ixgqo","title":"[ft-3tvvt] remove production storage writer_loop block_on bridge","description":"Follow-on from ft-3tvvt. storage.rs writer_loop creates a private current-thread runtime to block_on runtime_async::mpsc::Receiver::recv. Refactor to a small writer inbox bridge with a synchronous receive API, or move the writer loop to an async-owned confinement design, without direct tokio usage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:00:14.615961Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T21:15:01.459501Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T21:15:01.459283Z","close_reason":"Shipped (commit 3f0139bde, linter-co-committed). The audited block_on bridge at storage.rs:11574 (writer_loop's per-thread asupersync runtime + CompatRuntime::block_on(rx.recv(...))) is gone. Replaced with poll-and-park: try_recv() (sync, non-allocating, exposed by asupersync mpsc Receiver) drives the recv side, std::thread::sleep(1ms) handles the no-load wait. SQL dispatch was already sync; now the entire writer thread is sync end-to-end with no runtime dependency. Wake-up latency under no-load is bounded at 1ms (negligible vs SQLite per-statement autocommit cost). Channel close - whether by Disconnected (sender dropped) or Cancelled (cx-aware shutdown) - terminates the loop cleanly. Eliminates: (1) per-writer-thread runtime allocation waste, (2) the deadlock surface flagged by ft-3tvvt parent audit (library secretly owning a runtime), (3) the cancel-bypass concern (mpsc RecvError::Cancelled now naturally exits the loop). Test verification: storage::storage_handle_tests passes 40/40 (including writer_loop_does_not_dispatch_commands_queued_after_shutdown and storage_handle_shutdown_flushes_pending_writes), storage::queue_depth_tests passes 6/6, storage::storage_handle_tests::writer_loop_* passes 1/1 — 47 tests total. The other 8 block_on sites in storage.rs flagged by ft-3tvvt are test-only (per cod_4's audit summary in parent bead); this was the only production site.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-j0ufc","title":"[security-audit] workflow runner: detection on attacker-controlled pane output drives policy-gated actions on OTHER panes (privilege amplification)","description":"## Concrete attack

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs` is the event-
driven workflow execution loop. Workflows are triggered by
detection events (`crate::patterns::Detection`) which fire when
configured patterns match on pane output text.

The architecture diagram in runner.rs:243 shows:

```text
EventBus (detections) -> WorkflowRunner -> find_matching_workflow
                                        -> acquire_pane_lock
                                        -> WorkflowEngine (persist)
                                        -> execute_steps
                                        -> release_pane_lock
```

The execution-step body (line ~1354) calls
`wait_condition_pause_maybe_cx`, dispatches `Send` actions
through `CxPolicyInjector` — the action IS policy-gated on the
PANE the workflow is acting on.

**However:** the DETECTION input is attacker-controlled (any
agent's output text), and the workflow's ACTION pane is
DECOUPLED from the detection's source pane. A malicious agent
on pane A can produce output that matches a workflow trigger;
the workflow then drives an action on pane B (the configured
target).

This is **privilege amplification by pattern injection**:

- Pane A is operated by a low-privilege agent (e.g., a
  rate-limited tester).
- Pane B is operated by a high-privilege agent (e.g., the
  release pipeline).
- A workflow `handle_compaction` is configured: detection
  on `Compacting…` text → action `send_text("/continue")` to
  pane B.
- The low-privilege agent on pane A prints `Compacting…` (legit
  text in any console output).
- Workflow fires, sends `/continue` to pane B regardless of
  whether the operator authorized it for THIS event.

The `CxPolicyInjector` policy-gate fires on the SEND action,
but the policy input doesn't know "this trigger came from a
low-trust source pane." The workflow's pane-lock manager
prevents two workflows targeting pane B at once, but doesn't
prevent A→B causation.

## Why this is exploitable

Concrete scenarios:

1. **Cross-pane command injection.** A malicious agent in pane A
   prints output matching a workflow's trigger pattern; the
   workflow drives a command into pane B that pane B's operator
   never authorized.

2. **Rate-limit evasion.** Pane A is rate-limited at the
   PolicyEngine level (e.g., 30 sends/min). Pane A causes 100
   workflow firings, each of which sends to pane B without
   pane A's rate-limit budget being charged — only pane B's
   budget is consumed.

3. **Loop construction.** Pane A causes workflow on pane B,
   workflow's response in pane B matches another workflow's
   trigger that targets pane A — feedback loop, neither pane's
   operator initiated.

The skill's `⊙ Identity-Chain Trace` operator predicts this
exactly: the identity claim arrives at the workflow runner as
"pane A produced text X." The handler uses it for "execute
workflow targeting pane B." The claim is NOT cross-verified
against the workflow operator's authorization for that trigger
to drive that target. The claim IS attacker-controllable (any
agent's output is text). An attacker CAN craft a claim
(produce the matching text) pointing to a victim's authority
(workflow's target pane).

Per skill Axiom 6 ("Presence-only header checks are worthless"),
the workflow runner today does presence-only check: "did pattern
match? then trigger." It does NOT verify the trust relationship
between the source-pane and the action-pane.

## Suggested fix

1. **Add source-pane trust scoping to workflow triggers.** The
   workflow definition (`Workflow` trait, `handles(detection)`)
   should be ALLOWED to predicate on detection.pane_id and
   refuse triggers from untrusted source panes:

   ```rust
   pub struct WorkflowTriggerPolicy {
       pub allowed_source_panes: Option<HashSet<u64>>,
       pub require_user_actor: bool,
   }
   ```

2. **Audit log the (source_pane → target_pane) edge.** Today
   `record_to_decision_log` records the SEND action with target
   pane. It should also record the originating detection's
   source pane so post-incident forensics can trace cross-pane
   causation.

3. **Add a policy clause** "actions originating from workflow
   triggers on pane S require the originating S to have
   capability X" — an explicit chain-of-trust check.

4. **Document the threat model** in
   `docs/workflow-trust-boundaries.md`: workflow operators must
   know that ANY pane's output can drive their workflow unless
   they configure a source-pane allowlist.

## Out of scope

- The pattern engine itself (regex matching on agent output
  is by design).
- Disabling workflows entirely (they are core feature).
- Per-workflow IAM (separate larger design).

## Acceptance criteria

- `Workflow::handles(&detection)` API gains an optional
  source-pane check.
- The audit log records (source_pane, target_pane) edges, not
  just target pane.
- A regression test
  `tests/workflow_cross_pane_amplification.rs` proves a
  low-trust source pane CANNOT drive a workflow action on a
  high-trust pane when the workflow declares a source-pane
  allowlist.
- README's "Workflow" feature section adds a security-model
  note.

## References

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:240-260`
  (architecture comment)
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs:1354`
  (wait + send dispatch)
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns/Detection.rs` (the
  attacker-controllable input)
- Skill: security-audit-for-saas — `⊙ Identity-Chain Trace`
  operator + Axiom 6 (presence-only checks)

 {"id":"ft-j1qjt.1","title":"[ft-j1qjt.1] extract frankenterm-core-capture-types leaf crate (replay_capture + FtreplayArtifact types core depends on)","description":"Per docs/proposals/ft-j1qjt-replay-tier1-blocker.md: extract the 7 types core's policy/runtime/workflows currently reach into the replay cluster for (replay_capture::SharedCaptureAdapter, DecisionEvent, DecisionType, CollectingCaptureSink, CaptureAdapter, CaptureConfig + replay_fixture_harvest::FtreplayArtifact) into a leaf crate following the frankenterm-core-resource-types (ft-usvnt) pattern. Re-export from core under crate::replay_capture::* + crate::replay_fixture_harvest::* so the 20 inline call sites keep resolving. Prerequisite for ft-j1qjt.2.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:38:09.436288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:54:21.116179Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:54:21.115709Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","refactor","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-j1qjt.1","depends_on_id":"ft-j1qjt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:38:09.436288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
